content.problemStatement:
Problem Statement:

You are given a string S.
Your task is to reverse the string and print the reversed result.


Input Format
A single line containing the string S.



Output Format
Print the reversed string.
constraints: S contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string S consisting of lowercase English letters (the string may be empty).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output the reversed string on its own line, in the same order as the input test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are three test cases. Reversing each string gives "olleh", "dlrow" and "a" respectively.
A single empty string is reversed to an empty string, so the output line is blank.
First string "abc def" becomes "fed cba"; second string "123" becomes "321".

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each string, iterate from the last character to the first and output them in that order. Reversing can also be done with built‑in slicing (e.g., s[::-1] in Python) but the explicit loop makes the algorithm clear. The total work is proportional to the sum of lengths of all strings.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) – each character is visited exactly once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input and output buffers (the output can be built incrementally).

code.Python3:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
if not lines:
    sys.exit()
T = int(lines[0].strip())
out = []
for i in range(1, T+1):
    s = lines[i] if i < len(lines) else ''
    out.append(s[::-1])
sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
